subject: Chemistry
chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Elements

concepts.title: Metallurgy is the Process of Isolation of Metals
concepts.description: Metallurgy involves the process of extracting metals from their ores in a highly pure state. This includes various steps like crushing, grinding, and refining of ores to obtain the metal.

concepts.title: Pyrometallurgy and Hydrometallurgy
concepts.description: Pyrometallurgy involves high-temperature processes to extract metals, whereas Hydrometallurgy uses aqueous solutions to extract metals.

concepts.title: Roasting and Calcination
concepts.description: Roasting is the process of heating an ore in the presence of air to convert it into an oxide, while calcination involves heating an ore in the absence of air to convert it into an oxide.

concepts.title: Mnemonics for Metallurgical Processes
concepts.description: 'CART' stands for Crushing, Attrition, Roasting, and Thermal (Pyrometallurgy) or Chemical (Hydrometallurgy) Treatment to extract metals.

concepts.title: Confusing Calcination and Roasting
concepts.description: Students often confuse calcination and roasting. Remember that calcination is done in the absence of air, whereas roasting is done in the presence of air.

concepts.title: Units of Extraction of Metals
concepts.description: The units of extraction of metals include the percentage of metal in the ore, the cost of extraction, and the environmental impact of the process.

concepts.title: Refining of Metals
concepts.description: Refining of metals involves the removal of impurities from the extracted metal to obtain a highly pure product. This can be done through electrolysis, fractional distillation, or other methods.

concepts.title: Importance of Metals in Daily Life
concepts.description: Metals play a crucial role in our daily lives, being used in various applications such as construction, transportation, electronics, and more.
